steps.title: Map the two sentence parts
steps.content: The words before the blank can form a complete sentence: "American writer Edwidge Danticat, who emigrated from Haiti in 1981, has won acclaim for her powerful short stories, novels, and essays." The words after the blank introduce a new sentence about writer Robert Antoni comparing Danticat to Toni Morrison.

steps.title: Use a period before the phrase
steps.content: Because the first part is already an independent clause, it needs a real sentence boundary before the Robert Antoni sentence begins. A period after "essays" ends the first sentence. Then "Praising her lyrical yet unflinching depictions of her native country's turbulent history" introduces Antoni as the person doing the praising.

steps.title: Eliminate boundary errors
steps.content:
• Choice A - essays, praising: A comma alone cannot join the complete Danticat sentence to the complete Robert Antoni sentence, so this creates a comma splice.
  • Choice B - essays and praising: "And praising" is not parallel with the nouns "short stories, novels, and essays," and it still lacks a proper sentence boundary.
  • Choice C - essays praising: With no punctuation, the sentence parts run together incorrectly.
  • Choice D - essays. Praising: The period ends the first sentence, and "Praising" properly begins the introductory phrase for the Antoni sentence.
The answer is D.
